nodes.label: AI as collaborative second opinion for changing conditions
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or unnecessary for dynamic tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I often collaborate a lot with AI. The landscape is always changing, and I'd rather have a second opinion

nodes.label: AI as one-time lookup tool for parts
nodes.label_negative: AI as ongoing dependency for known information
nodes.grounding_span: once I know where those parts/pieces are, I never tend to use it for those tasks again

nodes.label: AI reliability and accuracy in calculations
nodes.label_negative: AI giving wild or incorrect numbers
nodes.grounding_span: I have received some wild numbers when inputting all information at once

nodes.label: Human judgment for finish work and safety-critical tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI delegation for finish work and safety-critical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: Any finish work or highly technical work I do without consulting AI

nodes.label: AI as productivity booster for faster job completion
nodes.label_negative: AI as unnecessary crutch that slows work
nodes.grounding_span: When you finish a job in half the quoted time, it feels pretty good

nodes.label: AI as tool for self-sufficiency and competence
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to job security or skill atrophy
nodes.grounding_span: It makes you look exceedingly competent when you don't have to ask the boss how to do something

nodes.grounding_span: I still take pride in getting as close to perfect as possible
